title: The Desert March Continues
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on the desert march continues, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Desert March Continues. Vivi and the Straw Hats decide that stopping the war means confronting Crocodile directly instead of chasing the rebels blindly. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Easy for you to say when you're riding a camel...!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yeah, that's right! Let us on too,

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: I alone could never make the decision to go to Rainbow...

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Once I kick Crocedile's ass, feed me until I explode.

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Why not!? I have a right to join the rebels like everyone else!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: This man here received that wound during a battle, while trying to cover me...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There's also the hospital ward and cemetery that I can show you...

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: I'm not scared of that stuff!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: My friend who lives in the town beside Erumalu is sick right now!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And I know it won't be long before his town also dries up like Erumalu!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: This is all because he stole our rain!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: So I want to fight too! Things like injuries or death isn't going to scare me away!

summary: This part develops the desert march continues and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
